title: nvm_read requests meta.len bytes even when offset > 0, exceeding object bounds
description: nvm_read fetches meta.len from nvm_metadata then calls wh_Client_NvmRead with data_len = meta.len regardless of offset. When offset = 5 and meta.len = 10, the call requests 10 bytes starting at offset 5, asking for bytes 5-14, but the object only has bytes 0-9. The server truncates out_len to 5, which is then used to truncate the Vec, so the returned data is correct. However, the passed data_len = meta.len is semantically wrong: the buffer should be meta.len - offset bytes (or saturating_sub to avoid underflow when offset >= meta.len), and that is also what should be passed as data_len to the C call.
